# Sapwood

> Shape your signer -- browser-based management UI for the Heartwood ESP32 signing device.

Sapwood connects to a Heartwood ESP32 hardware signer via Web Serial API (Chrome/Edge) and provides a management interface for TOFU client policies, OTA firmware updates, factory reset, and device monitoring. No server required -- it's a static SPA deployed to GitHub Pages.

## Key facts

- Static SPA: Svelte 5, 21KB gzipped, zero server dependencies
- Connects via Web Serial API directly to ESP32 USB-Serial-JTAG
- TypeScript port of the Heartwood frame protocol (19 tests, byte-compatible with Rust)
- Secrets never leave the ESP32 -- all destructive ops require physical button press
- Part of the Heartwood ecosystem: Heartwood (device) + Sapwood (management UI) + Bark (browser extension)

## Frame protocol

Frame format: [0x48 0x57] [type_u8] [length_u16_be] [payload...] [crc32_be32]
CRC32 (IEEE 802.3) covers type + length bytes + payload.

Key frame types:
- 0x05 PROVISION_LIST (empty) -> 0x07 PROVISION_LIST_RESPONSE (JSON)
- 0x24 FACTORY_RESET (empty, requires button)
- 0x27 POLICY_LIST_REQUEST (slot byte) -> 0x28 POLICY_LIST_RESPONSE (JSON)
- 0x29 POLICY_REVOKE (slot + 64-char hex pubkey)
- 0x2A POLICY_UPDATE (slot + JSON ClientPolicy)
- 0x30-0x32 OTA_BEGIN/CHUNK/FINISH -> 0x33 OTA_STATUS

## Source

- Repository: https://github.com/forgesworn/sapwood
- Live: https://forgesworn.github.io/sapwood/
- Frame protocol: src/lib/frame.ts
- Serial transport: src/lib/serial.ts
- Device state: src/lib/device.svelte.ts
